description: Narration configuration reconstructed directly from the two real narration formulas found in 'Copy of AMB Bank Statements 2026-27.xlsx': (1) the Google-Sheets LET-based formula used in AMB MASTER KVB-6375 / AMB RERA BOM 667 / AMB CA AXIS-280 / KVB FREE 1050 (fully resolved example available), and (2) the native Excel TRIM/IF formula used only in IDW KVB-6535 (formula text inspected, referenced separately below, NOT used as the base template since it hardcodes a single fixed counter-account name and is not generalizable across accounts via placeholders). Not wired into any script yet.

observed_formula_reference.style_b_source: AMB MASTER KVB-6375 Narration column (LET-based formula), applies to 4 of 5 bank sheets
observed_formula_reference.style_b_resolved_example: Being amount received from DIMPAL (AMBITION COLONISERS PRIVATE LTD) in AMB MASTER KVB - 6375 towards Collection vide RTGS CR PSIB0020974 (PSIBR22026040700701222) (Business Unit: SW | Head: Collection | Type: Customer Collection) (Unit No: SWC-036)
observed_formula_reference.style_a_source: IDW KVB-6535 Narration column (native Excel TRIM/IF formula) — sheet-specific, hardcodes 'YES MASTER 0264' as the counter-account; documented for transparency but not adopted as the base template family
observed_formula_reference.note: All templates below follow Style B's structure since it is the dominant, fully-resolved, head-agnostic pattern across most of the workbook. Only 'Collection' has a live resolved example confirming exact wording; all other heads apply the same formula structure with {head} substituted, which is faithful to the formula logic itself (the formula takes Head as a variable), not an invented style.

Internal.credit_template: Being amount transferred from {party} ({account_no}) to {bank_account} vide {reference}{note} (Business Unit: {business_unit} | Head: {head} | Type: {type})
Internal.debit_template: Being amount transferred to {party} ({account_no}) from {bank_account} vide {reference}{note} (Business Unit: {business_unit} | Head: {head} | Type: {type})
Internal.fallback_template: Banking transaction of {amount} recorded under {head} (Business Unit: {business_unit}, Ref: {reference}).
